summary: Renamed a broad set of unknown HSD/sysdolphin baselib symbols, mostly in video and render state code, and propagated those names through assembly callsites and a small number of C/header references. The PR body says "Mostly video, state, and lobj"; the diff evidence strongly shows HSD VI/video names, HSD state/PE/TEV helper names, and AXDriver aux allocation globals.
what_changed: Replaced the video render-mode data label lbl_804C1D80 with HSD_VIData across HSD video code and Melee callers, including db, gm, baselib_unknown_002, displayfunc/video, and C code that now references extern GXRenderModeObj HSD_VIData.

Named VI/video functions such as HSD_VICopyXFB, HSD_VISetBlack, HSD_VICopyEFB2XFBPtr, HSD_VIGetDrawDoneWaitingFlag, HSD_VIGetXFBDrawEnable, HSD_VIPreRetraceCB, HSD_VIPostRetraceCB, and HSD_VIGXDrawDoneCB.

Named many HSD state functions in asm/sysdolphin/baselib/state.s, including HSD_StateSetBlendMode, HSD_StateSetZMode, HSD_StateSetAlphaCompare, HSD_StateSetColorUpdate, HSD_StateSetAlphaUpdate, HSD_StateSetDstAlpha, HSD_StateSetZCompLoc, HSD_StateSetDither, HSD_StateInvalidate, HSD_StateGetNumTevStages, HSD_StateSetNumTevStages, and HSD_SetTevRegAll.

Propagated HSD_StateInvalidate callsite names widely, replacing func_80361FC4 in fighter, game mode, ground display, item, collision, refract, map, psdisp, sislib, sobjlib, tobj, and other assembly files.
Propagated other state helper callsite names such as HSD_StateSetZMode, HSD_StateSetZCompLoc, HSD_StateSetColorUpdate, HSD_StateSetNumTevStages, and HSD_SetTevRegAll.

Added or updated state prototypes in include/sysdolphin/baselib/state.h, including HSD_StateSetZMode(u32, u32, u32) and HSD_StateInvalidate(s32), enabling C references in files such as ftdrawcommon.c and gmmain.c.
Renamed AXDriver small-data globals lbl_804D77D8 and lbl_804D77DC to axfxallocsize and axfxmaxsize, with uses updated in AXDriverAlloc and AXDriver setup/reset paths.
smart_moves: The PR treated known HSD baselib names as shared infrastructure and renamed both definitions and all visible callsites, which makes later decompilation of unrelated Melee systems easier because render-state calls become self-describing.
It prioritized high-fanout symbols such as HSD_StateInvalidate and HSD_VIData, yielding broad readability improvement with no apparent code-generation or behavior changes.
It paired assembly renames with minimal header/C updates where C code needed the same names, avoiding a split-brain situation where asm and C used different labels for the same HSD functions/data.
The AXDriver global names were inferred from repeated allocation-size/max-size usage patterns in AXDriverAlloc and aux setup code rather than left as raw lbl_ small-data names.
decomp_lessons: When a baselib function is identified, propagate the name through every asm callsite immediately; high-fanout HSD state helpers appear throughout fighter, item, ground, collision, map, and text/video code.

Global render-mode data like HSD_VIData is recognizable by repeated indexed accesses, VI/video initialization usage, and C references as GXRenderModeObj; once named, it clarifies both video setup and caller code.

State wrapper functions around GX PE/TEV operations should be named semantically by the GX state they manage: color update, alpha update, dst alpha, blend mode, Z mode, Z compare location, dither, TEV stage count, and TEV registers.
Small-data labels can often be named by watching load/store flows: axfxallocsize is accumulated and compared against axfxmaxsize in AXDriverAlloc, while setup/reset code initializes both.
A naming-only PR can be valuable decomp groundwork even when it does not add C matches, because it reduces anonymous func_/lbl_ noise and gives future matching attempts stable semantic anchors.
naming_conventions: HSD baselib functions use HSD_ prefix plus subsystem/verb style, e.g. HSD_StateInvalidate, HSD_StateSetZMode, HSD_VICopyXFB, HSD_VISetBlack.
HSD state setters use HSD_StateSet* for cached render-state mutations and HSD_StateGet* for queries, e.g. HSD_StateSetNumTevStages and HSD_StateGetNumTevStages.
VI/video callbacks and helpers use HSD_VI* names, e.g. HSD_VIPreRetraceCB, HSD_VIPostRetraceCB, HSD_VIGXDrawDoneCB, and HSD_VICopyEFB2XFBPtr.
Shared HSD global data is named as a semantic object rather than an address label, e.g. lbl_804C1D80 became HSD_VIData.
AXDriver aux-effect allocation globals were named in compact lowercase style, axfxallocsize and axfxmaxsize, matching their apparent AXFX allocation role.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Most edits are pure symbol substitutions in assembly comments/instructions, preserving addresses, opcodes, branch targets, and instruction sequences.
Callsite renames map old unknown function labels directly to new HSD names, for example func_80361FC4 to HSD_StateInvalidate and func_80361BB8 to HSD_StateSetZMode.
Data-label renames update both @ha and @l relocation uses, as with lbl_804C1D80 to HSD_VIData and AXDriver small-data labels to axfxallocsize/axfxmaxsize.
Header prototypes were added only where needed for C callsites, keeping the PR primarily a naming pass rather than a structural rewrite.
The diff shows no evidence of algorithmic changes; matching safety came from leaving generated code bytes and control flow intact while changing symbol names.
review_feedback: One review by ribbanya approved the PR with no body text.
There were no issue comments or inline review comments in the provided slice, so no detailed reviewer rationale is available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile asm/sysdolphin/baselib/state.s using HSD_StateInvalidate or HSD_StateSetZMode names.
Find later PRs that define the structure or fields behind HSD_VIData and GXRenderModeObj usage.
Search for remaining func_80361FC4, func_80361BB8, func_803761C0, func_803767A0, and lbl_804C1D80 references after this PR.
Investigate whether the PR body's mention of lobj corresponds to symbols not visible in the excerpt or to related baselib object naming in nearby files.
